title: rubus genevieri var. obliquatus
intro: Rubus genevieri var. obliquatus, commonly known as the Alpine Bramble or Thimbleberry, is a type of shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its leaves, stems, and roots. The health benefits of Rubus genevieri var. obliquatus include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to leverage its medicinal properties.
